cells.query:
-- Stress analysis
--
-- Starter notebook for agent-sandbox stress-test artifacts loaded by
-- stress-duckdb. Views (v_runs, v_launch_latency, …) are created on
-- register/pr. Run cells top-to-bottom; tweak run_id filters as needed.

SELECT table_name
FROM information_schema.tables
WHERE table_schema = 'main'
ORDER BY table_name;

-- Headline numbers per run (the trend table).
SELECT * FROM v_runs;

-- Where does launch time go? Segment p50 (ms) by phase.
-- scheduled→running is the node-side pod start cost;
-- under churn it used to dominate, then deletion took over.
SELECT phase,
       count(*) AS n,
       round(quantile_cont(createAckMs, 0.50), 0) AS ack_p50,
       round(quantile_cont(podCreatedMs - createAckMs, 0.50), 0) AS to_pod_p50,
       round(quantile_cont(podScheduledMs - podCreatedMs, 0.50), 0) AS to_sched_p50,
       round(quantile_cont(podRunningMs - podScheduledMs, 0.50), 0) AS sched_to_run_p50,
       round(quantile_cont(podReadyMs - podRunningMs, 0.50), 0) AS run_to_ready_p50,
       round(quantile_cont(sandboxReadyMs - podReadyMs, 0.50), 0) AS ready_to_sb_p50,
       round(quantile_cont(sandboxReadyMs, 0.50), 0) AS e2e_p50,
       round(quantile_cont(sandboxReadyMs, 0.90), 0) AS e2e_p90
FROM sandboxes
WHERE sandboxReadyMs IS NOT NULL
  AND podRunningMs IS NOT NULL
  AND podScheduledMs IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY phase
ORDER BY phase;

-- Deletion cost: deleteCalled → pod gone from the API.
-- Under churn this has been the cycle-time ceiling (~9s p50),
-- mostly waiting on kube-controller-manager garbage collection.
SELECT * FROM v_deletion;

-- KCM garbage-collector workqueue depth by phase.
-- A deep/saturated GC queue during throughput supports the
-- "churn ceiling is GC/KCM QPS, not per-node start" hypothesis.
SELECT p.phase,
       g.queue,
       count(*) AS samples,
       round(avg(g.depth), 1) AS avg_depth,
       max(g.depth) AS max_depth
FROM v_gc_queue_depth g
JOIN phases p
  ON g.run_id = p.run_id
 AND g.ts::TIMESTAMPTZ BETWEEN p.start_ts AND p.end_ts
GROUP BY p.phase, g.queue
ORDER BY p.phase, g.queue;

-- GC queue depth over time (latest run), for charting.
WITH latest AS (SELECT run_id FROM v_runs ORDER BY run_ts DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT g.ts::TIMESTAMPTZ AS ts,
       p.phase,
       g.queue,
       g.depth
FROM v_gc_queue_depth g
JOIN latest USING (run_id)
LEFT JOIN phases p
  ON g.run_id = p.run_id
 AND g.ts::TIMESTAMPTZ BETWEEN p.start_ts AND p.end_ts
ORDER BY ts;

-- All workqueue depths during throughput (controller + KCM + …).
-- Look for sandbox / garbage_collector queues climbing.
SELECT p.phase,
       w.source,
       w.queue,
       round(avg(w.depth), 1) AS avg_depth,
       max(w.depth) AS max_depth
FROM v_workqueue_depth w
JOIN phases p
  ON w.run_id = p.run_id
 AND w.ts::TIMESTAMPTZ BETWEEN p.start_ts AND p.end_ts
WHERE p.phase = 'throughput'
GROUP BY p.phase, w.source, w.queue
HAVING max(w.depth) > 0
ORDER BY max_depth DESC;

-- Ready throughput from the per-second timeseries (latest run).
-- Compare overall vs best windows in summary; this shows the shape.
WITH latest AS (SELECT run_id FROM v_runs ORDER BY run_ts DESC LIMIT 1),
raw AS (
  SELECT t.offsetSeconds,
         t.sandboxReady,
         t.sandboxReady - lag(t.sandboxReady) OVER (ORDER BY t.offsetSeconds) AS ready_delta,
         t.livePods
  FROM timeseries t
  JOIN latest USING (run_id)
)
SELECT offsetSeconds,
       ready_delta AS ready_per_s,
       livePods
FROM raw
WHERE ready_delta IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY offsetSeconds;

-- Per-node ready count in throughput (load balance / hot nodes).
SELECT nodeName,
       count(*) AS ready_n,
       round(quantile_cont(sandboxReadyMs, 0.50), 0) AS e2e_p50_ms,
       round(quantile_cont(podRunningMs - podScheduledMs, 0.50), 0) AS sched_to_run_p50_ms
FROM sandboxes
WHERE phase = 'throughput'
  AND sandboxReadyMs IS NOT NULL
  AND nodeName IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY nodeName
ORDER BY ready_n DESC;

-- Apiserver request rate by verb during throughput (latest run).
-- Useful when checking whether churn is API-QPS bound.
WITH latest AS (SELECT run_id FROM v_runs ORDER BY run_ts DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT r.metric,
       r.labels['verb'] AS verb,
       r.labels['resource'] AS resource,
       round(avg(r.rate_per_s), 1) AS avg_rate,
       round(max(r.rate_per_s), 1) AS max_rate
FROM v_metric_rate r
JOIN latest USING (run_id)
JOIN phases p
  ON r.run_id = p.run_id
 AND r.ts::TIMESTAMPTZ BETWEEN p.start_ts AND p.end_ts
WHERE p.phase = 'throughput'
  AND r.source = 'kube-apiserver'
  AND r.metric = 'apiserver_request_total'
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
HAVING max(r.rate_per_s) > 1
ORDER BY max_rate DESC
LIMIT 40;

-- Watch stream coverage (pods / nodes / events / sandboxes).
SELECT resource, type AS event_type, count(*) AS n
FROM watch
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1, 2;

-- Latest pod phase per name (from watch), latest run.
WITH latest AS (SELECT run_id FROM v_runs ORDER BY run_ts DESC LIMIT 1),
ranked AS (
  SELECT *,
         row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY namespace, name ORDER BY ts DESC) AS rn
  FROM v_watch_pods
  JOIN latest USING (run_id)
)
SELECT namespace, name, node_name, phase, event_type, ts, container_id
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY phase, name
LIMIT 100;

-- Node inventory from watch (latest observation per node).
WITH latest AS (SELECT run_id FROM v_runs ORDER BY run_ts DESC LIMIT 1),
ranked AS (
  SELECT *,
         row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY name ORDER BY ts DESC) AS rn
  FROM v_watch_nodes
  JOIN latest USING (run_id)
)
SELECT name, kubelet_version, capacity_pods, allocatable_pods, event_type, ts
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY name;

-- Warning/Error Events during throughput (latest run).
WITH latest AS (SELECT run_id FROM v_runs ORDER BY run_ts DESC LIMIT 1)
SELECT e.ts, e.namespace, e.involved_kind, e.involved_name, e.reason, e.k8s_type, e.message
FROM v_watch_events e
JOIN latest USING (run_id)
JOIN phases p
  ON e.run_id = p.run_id
 AND e.ts BETWEEN p.start_ts AND p.end_ts
WHERE p.phase = 'throughput'
  AND e.k8s_type IN ('Warning', 'Error')
ORDER BY e.ts
LIMIT 200;
